title: Microsoft Graph API - Attack Simulation Training Endpoints
applies_to: Microsoft Graph security attack simulation endpoints

confirmed_facts.fact: Microsoft Graph v1.0 attackSimulationRoot resource exposes collections: simulations, simulationAutomations, trainings, payloads, loginPages, landingPages, endUserNotifications, and operations.
List simulations: GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/security/attackSimulation/simulations
List simulations supports $count, $filter, $orderby, $skipToken, $top, $select query params
Filterable properties on simulations: attackTechnique, attackType, completionDateTime, displayName, isAutomated, launchDateTime, status
Simulation object key fields: id, displayName, description, status, launchDateTime, completionDateTime, isAutomated, automationId, attackTechnique, attackType, payloadDeliveryPlatform
Per-simulation detail: GET /v1.0/security/attackSimulation/simulations/{simulationId}
Campaign overview metrics: GET /v1.0/security/attackSimulation/simulations/{simulationId}/report/overview

simulationReportOverview fields: resolvedTargetsCount (Int32), simulationEventsContent (phishingSimulationEventsContent sub-object with click/compromise rates), trainingEventsContent (training completion metrics), and recommendedActions (array of attack simulation recommended action objects).
User results: GET /v1.0/security/attackSimulation/simulations/{simulationId}/report/simulationUsers
simulationUsers endpoint only supports $count, $skipToken, $top — does NOT support $filter, $select, or $orderby
Both list and simulationUsers endpoints require AttackSimulation.Read.All application permission
Admin consent is required for AttackSimulation.Read.All in both delegated and application permission modes.
API is GA (v1.0) — no beta dependency required
Pagination uses @odata.nextLink with $skipToken — do NOT use $skip
Attack Simulation Training workload requires Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft Defender for Office 365 Plan 2; only a limited subset is available via E3 trial.
Attack sim Graph APIs are documented as unavailable in GCC High and DoD environments; the product exists in those clouds but without Graph API access.
Attack Simulation Training is available in Worldwide, GCC, GCC High, and DoD environments, but Graph API access is only confirmed for Worldwide and GCC.
Admin roles for Attack Simulation Training: Security Administrator or Attack Simulation Administrator for configuration; Security Reader and Security Operator can view all simulation data.
Attack Simulation Training supports on-premises mailboxes with reduced reporting functionality — user-level simulation detail may be incomplete in hybrid environments.

userSimulationDetails top-level fields: isCompromised (Boolean), compromisedDateTime (DateTimeOffset), reportedPhishDateTime (DateTimeOffset), assignedTrainingsCount (Int32), completedTrainingsCount (Int32), inProgressTrainingsCount (Int32)
userSimulationDetails.simulationUser sub-object fields: userId (String — Entra user ID), displayName (String), email (String)

userSimulationDetails.simulationEvents[] sub-object fields: eventName (String — e.g. SuccessfullyDeliveredEmail, EmailLinkClicked, ReportedPhish), eventDateTime (DateTimeOffset), ipAddress (String), osPlatformDeviceDetails (String), browser (String)

userSimulationDetails.trainingEvents[] sub-object fields: displayName (String — training name), latestTrainingStatus (Enum: unknown, assigned, inProgress, completed, overdue, unknownFutureValue), trainingAssignedProperties (userTrainingContentEventInfo), trainingUpdatedProperties (userTrainingContentEventInfo), trainingCompletedProperties (userTrainingContentEventInfo)

implementation_notes: Primary data retrieval shape: start with simulations list (id, displayName, status, dates), then for each simulation pull report/overview for summary metrics and report/simulationUsers for per-user detail.
Lookup strategy: first try $filter=displayName eq '{name}', if special characters cause issues, list all and match client-side
OData $filter with special characters (parentheses, slashes) may need URL encoding — have a fallback. Example: $filter=displayName eq 'AutomatedSimulation_Oct25 (2)_2/28/2026'
Pagination: check for '@odata.nextLink' in each response, GET that URL for next page, loop until absent. Use $top=100 for reasonable page sizes.

userSimulationDetails contains nested objects: flatten simulationUser fields to top level, serialize simulationEvents as individual rows or summarize key events (SuccessfullyDeliveredEmail, EmailLinkClicked, ReportedPhish), summarize training status from trainingEvents[].latestTrainingStatus.

Error handling: 401 (token expired), 403 (insufficient permissions), 404 (simulation not found), 429 (throttling — respect Retry-After header). Errors in standard OData format: {error: {code, message}}.
caveats: simulationUsers endpoint does NOT support $filter or $select — all filtering must be done client-side
Simulation displayName may contain special characters that break OData $filter — always have a client-side fallback
trainingEvents.latestTrainingStatus enum: unknown, assigned, inProgress, completed, overdue, unknownFutureValue
Attack sim Graph APIs are unavailable in GCC High and DoD — do not document these as viable cloud targets for Graph-based retrieval.
Licensing prerequisite: the workload requires M365 E5 or Defender for Office 365 Plan 2; the API returns no data or errors if the tenant lacks the license.
On-premises mailbox support exists but with reduced reporting functionality; user-level simulation detail may be incomplete in hybrid environments.
